positioning_statement.value: Linear is the product development system for high-velocity product and engineering organizations that need one focused operating layer for planning, building, customer feedback, and progress visibility.

evidence.claim: Linear describes itself as a product development system for teams and agents to plan, build, and monitor work.
OpenAI's story describes cross-team handoffs, stale statuses, and disconnected systems as collaboration problems.
Linear groups product features across issues, projects, initiatives, cycles, views, AI, integrations, and analytics.

one_line_promise.value: Turn fragmented product work into a clear system for planning, execution, feedback, and momentum.

evidence.claim: Customer Requests can connect feedback from support tools, CRM tools, email, and Slack to issues and projects.
The strongest intersection is scaling product delivery: ICP pain centers on handoffs and stale statuses, while the offer connects issues, projects, requests, and monitoring.

ranked_value_props.value: One operating layer for product delivery instead of disconnected issue, roadmap, feedback, and status systems.

evidence.claim: OpenAI's story describes cross-team handoffs, stale statuses, and disconnected systems as collaboration problems.
Linear groups product features across issues, projects, initiatives, cycles, views, AI, integrations, and analytics.

icp_fit_reason.value: This maps directly to product and engineering teams whose core pain is fragmented coordination across handoffs, planning, feedback, and delivery.

evidence.claim: The strongest intersection is scaling product delivery: ICP pain centers on handoffs and stale statuses, while the offer connects issues, projects, requests, and monitoring.

objection_addressed.value: The value prop should acknowledge workflow-fit review instead of implying a frictionless migration.

evidence.claim: Enterprise evaluation may involve SAML, SCIM, granular admin controls, invoice or PO billing, and onboarding support.

ranked_value_props.value: A planning-to-progress system that helps leaders connect roadmap work, specs, quarterly planning, and execution visibility.

evidence.claim: Ramp uses Linear for roadmap visibility, spec lists, quarterly planning, and monthly check-ins.
Linear groups product features across issues, projects, initiatives, cycles, views, AI, integrations, and analytics.

icp_fit_reason.value: Product and design leaders need roadmap visibility and planning context to stay connected to execution.

evidence.claim: Ramp uses Linear for roadmap visibility, spec lists, quarterly planning, and monthly check-ins.

objection_addressed.value: Tie the promise to planning visibility and admin controls, not to unsupported productivity numbers.

evidence.claim: Enterprise evaluation may involve SAML, SCIM, granular admin controls, invoice or PO billing, and onboarding support.

ranked_value_props.value: Customer feedback becomes actionable product work instead of staying trapped in support, CRM, email, or chat systems.

evidence.claim: Customer Requests can connect feedback from support tools, CRM tools, email, and Slack to issues and projects.
Customer stories repeatedly frame the before-state as disconnected systems, coordination friction, and planning visibility gaps.

icp_fit_reason.value: Feedback intake is a named buying trigger and a visible product workflow for product teams.

evidence.claim: Customer Requests can connect feedback from support tools, CRM tools, email, and Slack to issues and projects.

ranked_value_props.value: Enterprise-ready evaluation can be framed around controls, billing, onboarding, and governance rather than only product speed.

evidence.claim: Enterprise evaluation may involve SAML, SCIM, granular admin controls, invoice or PO billing, and onboarding support.

icp_fit_reason.value: Larger teams evaluating a product development system need the migration and control story to match their rollout constraints.

evidence.claim: Enterprise evaluation may involve SAML, SCIM, granular admin controls, invoice or PO billing, and onboarding support.

objection_addressed.value: This directly addresses admin, security, billing, and onboarding evaluation requirements.

evidence.claim: Enterprise evaluation may involve SAML, SCIM, granular admin controls, invoice or PO billing, and onboarding support.

old_way.value: The old way is product work split across issue trackers, chat, spreadsheets, support tools, and separate planning artifacts.

evidence.claim: OpenAI's story describes cross-team handoffs, stale statuses, and disconnected systems as collaboration problems.
Customer stories repeatedly frame the before-state as disconnected systems, coordination friction, and planning visibility gaps.

cost_of_old_way.value: The cost is not a sourced numeric loss; it is visible coordination drag: stale status, harder handoffs, and feedback disconnected from delivery.

evidence.claim: The strongest intersection is scaling product delivery: ICP pain centers on handoffs and stale statuses, while the offer connects issues, projects, requests, and monitoring.
Customer Requests can connect feedback from support tools, CRM tools, email, and Slack to issues and projects.

new_way.value: The new way is a product development system where planning, building, monitoring, feedback, and AI-assisted workflows share one operating context.

evidence.claim: Linear describes itself as a product development system for teams and agents to plan, build, and monitor work.
Linear groups product features across issues, projects, initiatives, cycles, views, AI, integrations, and analytics.

proof_bridge.value: OpenAI is proof for the scale and handoff problem, while Ramp is proof for planning visibility and roadmap operations.

evidence.claim: OpenAI adopted Linear across thousands of users as teams scaled and needed alignment across complex dependencies.
Ramp uses Linear for roadmap visibility, spec lists, quarterly planning, and monthly check-ins.

proof_bridge.value: The product proof bridge is the connection between issues, projects, initiatives, customer requests, integrations, analytics, and AI workflows.

evidence.claim: Linear groups product features across issues, projects, initiatives, cycles, views, AI, integrations, and analytics.

closing_frame.value: Position Linear as the operating context for modern product delivery, not merely a faster issue tracker.

evidence.claim: Positioning should emphasize operating-model clarity over generic issue tracking.

status_quo_contrast.value: Status quo: product decisions and delivery status live in separate tools. Linear frame: one system connects planning, work, monitoring, and feedback.

evidence.claim: OpenAI's story describes cross-team handoffs, stale statuses, and disconnected systems as collaboration problems.
Linear groups product features across issues, projects, initiatives, cycles, views, AI, integrations, and analytics.

status_quo_contrast.value: Status quo: customer feedback stays detached from delivery. Linear frame: feedback can become issues and projects tied to product work.

evidence.claim: Customer Requests can connect feedback from support tools, CRM tools, email, and Slack to issues and projects.

status_quo_contrast.value: Status quo: enterprise evaluation gets reduced to tool comparison. Linear frame: evaluation can address controls, billing, onboarding, and governance requirements.

evidence.claim: Enterprise evaluation may involve SAML, SCIM, granular admin controls, invoice or PO billing, and onboarding support.

claims_not_allowed.value: Do not use quantified shipping-speed guarantees without sourced metric evidence.

evidence.claim: No quantified performance guarantee is locked in the brief.

claims_not_allowed.value: Do not claim AI removes product leadership or planning responsibility; frame AI as part of product workflows only when sourced.

evidence.claim: Linear groups product features across issues, projects, initiatives, cycles, views, AI, integrations, and analytics.

claims_not_allowed.value: Do not reduce the strategy to platform recommendations, budget allocation, scripts, keyword plans, or launch tasks.

evidence.claim: Positioning should emphasize operating-model clarity over generic issue tracking.
